title: Reality Check: 2025 Projection Audit
description: An analysis of ESPN's preseason fantasy football projections against actual 2025 results - 191 players, 4 positions, 18 weeks of data revealing which players boomed and busted.

detail.tagline: An audit of how ESPN's 2025 preseason fantasy projections held up against what actually happened.
detail.role: Solo — Python data pipeline, analysis, and the front-end story.
overview: Every fantasy draft leans on preseason projections, but nobody goes back to check whether they were any good. Reality Check does — a scrollytelling audit of ESPN's 2025 preseason numbers against what all 191 audited players actually did across the season.

The finding: projections run hot. More than half the pool fell short, ESPN bakes optimism into the mid-rounds and caution into the bargain bin, and wide receivers were the most over-projected group on the board. The piece walks from that headline down to a sortable, per-player data room — and separates 'played badly' from 'never played,' because an injury breaks a season total no projection could price in.

gallery.caption: Gut Check — the headline: 191 audited players, four positions, and the finding that 55% fell short of their projection.
The mid-round dead zone — beat rate by projected tier shows ESPN over-projects the mid-rounds (only 24% beat) and under-projects cheap fliers.
Outliers — separating players who played badly from those who got hurt, by reading points per game actually played.
Player Mapping — chart any of 191 players' real week-by-week scoring against ESPN's flat preseason line.
Data Room — the whole board: every audited player, projected vs. actual, with consistency, boom/bust, and volatility (CV).

features.title: A guided data story
features.detail: Six numbered chapters take you from the headline miss rate down to individual players, each chart annotated with its takeaway.

features.title: Beat rate by tier
features.detail: Groups players by projected PPG to show ESPN systematically over-projects the mid-rounds and under-projects cheap fliers.

features.title: Availability vs. performance
features.detail: Separates busts who played badly from those who got hurt, reading points per game actually played — not spread across a full season.

features.title: Per-player weekly map
features.detail: Pick any of 191 players to chart their real week-by-week scoring against ESPN's flat preseason line.

features.title: The whole board
features.detail: A searchable, sortable table of every audited player with consistency, boom/bust counts, and week-to-week volatility (CV).

challenges.title: Two data worlds that don't line up
challenges.problem: Preseason projections live in a marketing PDF; actual results live in a stats API — different names, formats, and player universes that must be reconciled before anything can be compared.
challenges.outcome: A Python pipeline parses the projection PDF, pulls real stats from the ESPN API, and merges them into one keyed dataset with per-player deltas — so every comparison rests on matched, deduplicated records.

challenges.title: Averages hide the story
challenges.problem: A steady 15-PPG grinder and a boom-or-bust gambler can post the identical average, and a season total collapses the moment a star misses games — so raw projected-vs-actual numbers mislead.
challenges.outcome: The analysis adds per-game (not per-season) rates, week-to-week volatility via coefficient of variation, and an availability flag, so the audit distinguishes a bad projection from a bad-luck injury.

challenges.title: An analysis people will actually read
challenges.problem: A CSV of projection errors convinces nobody; the insight only lands if the reader can follow the argument and poke at the data themselves.
challenges.outcome: The findings ship as a numbered scrollytelling narrative — annotated charts that build to a conclusion — backed by an interactive per-player explorer and a full data table for anyone who wants to verify it.

stack.name: Python + pandas
stack.why: Merges projections and actuals into one dataset with per-player deltas and volatility.

stack.name: ESPN API
stack.why: Source of real 2025 weekly and season results.

stack.name: PDF parsing
stack.why: Extracts ESPN's preseason projections from their published sheet.

stack.name: React
stack.why: Interactive charts, the per-player explorer, and the sortable data room.
